summary: Q2-F1 remains a trigger-metadata and filtering bypass, not a new arbitrary-instruction delivery path. The trigger sanitizer accepts compatibility-form and hidden-character instruction strings under NFC, but this path stores/indexes metadata rather than emitting recovered prompt content.
attackScenario: attacker plants visually disguised instruction text in source content
trigger extraction keeps the phrase because sanitizeTriggerPhrase only uses NFC
the phrase survives as trigger metadata / quality-review input even though stronger surfaces interpret it as ASCII contamination
impact is poisoned metadata and inconsistent filtering semantics, not direct model instruction execution through this path

summary: sanitizeRecoveredPayload reaches only a shape-level Zod boundary. Hook-state validation requires pendingCompactPrime.payload to be a string, but no downstream schema re-validates the payload semantically after normalization.
note: Sanitizer misses therefore pass through unchanged, while caught lines are stripped without any content-aware schema boundary in between.

summary: The tested PoC corpus is stable across JSON serialize/deserialize around NFKC in the current Node runtime; no JSON round-trip drift was observed.
evidence: local Node runtime probe against current-main normalization rules

summary: No new P0 escalation was found for Q2-F1 in this iteration. The practical P0 still depends on the recovered-payload sanitizer miss already captured as Q2-F3, not on the trigger-phrase path alone.
